name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: It was too far to make out the banners clearly, but even through the drifting fog she could see that they were white, with a dark smudge in their center that could only be the direwolf of Stark, grey upon its icy field. When she saw it with her own eyes, Catelyn reined up her horse and bowed her head in thanks. The gods were good. She was not too late.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn穿过fog亲眼辨出Stark direwolf banners，感谢gods自己尚未迟到。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 亲眼看到banners把raven/rumor中的army变成物理事实；not too late同时指赶上Robb与还能影响战略。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “They await our coming, my lady,” Ser Wylis Manderly said, “as my lord father swore they would.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Wylis说army正等她，正如Wyman保证。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Let us not keep them waiting any longer, ser.” Ser Brynden Tully put the spurs to his horse and trotted briskly toward the banners. Catelyn rode beside him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Brynden催马前往，Catelyn并骑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Ser Wylis and his brother Ser Wendel followed, leading their levies, near fifteen hundred men: some twenty-odd knights and as many squires, two hundred mounted lances, swordsmen, and freeriders, and the rest foot, armed with spears, pikes and tridents. Lord Wyman had remained behind to see to the defenses of White Harbor. A man of near sixty years, he had grown too stout to sit a horse. “If I had thought to see war again in my lifetime, I should have eaten a few less eels,” he’d told Catelyn when he met her ship, slapping his massive belly with both hands. His fingers were fat as sausages. “My boys will see you safe to your son, though, have no fear.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Manderly兄弟带约一千五百men；年老肥胖Wyman留守White Harbor防务。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 兵力分项显示Manderly contribution质量：少量knights、mounted lances，大部分foot；Wyman留守也合理分配home defense。
blocks.background_zh: **Manderly levies：** White Harbor提供约一千五百人；Wyman本人留守重要port与North东海防务。

blocks.original_text: His “boys” were both older than Catelyn, and she might have wished that they did not take after their father quite so closely. Ser Wylis was only a few eels short of not being able to mount his own horse; she pitied the poor animal. Ser Wendel, the younger boy, would have been the fattest man she’d ever known, had she only neglected to meet his father and brother. Wylis was quiet and formal, Wendel loud and boisterous; both had ostentatious walrus mustaches and heads as bare as a baby’s bottom; neither seemed to own a single garment that was not spotted with food stains. Yet she liked them well enough; they had gotten her to Robb, as their father had vowed, and nothing else mattered.
blocks.explanation_zh: Wylis、Wendel虽同样肥胖邋遢，却履行护送承诺，因此Catelyn喜欢他们。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She was pleased to see that her son had sent eyes out, even to the east. The Lannisters would come from the south when they came, but it was good that Robb was being careful. My son is leading a host to war, she thought, still only half believing it. She was desperately afraid for him, and for Winterfell, yet she could not deny feeling a certain pride as well. A year ago he had been a boy. What was he now? she wondered.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她欣慰Robb向east派out­riders，恐惧又骄傲地问一年间son从boy变成了什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: east scouts虽敌军来自south，显示Robb在防unexpected approach；Catelyn pride与fear并存而不互相取消。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Outriders spied the Manderly banners—the white merman with trident in hand, rising from a blue-green sea—and hailed them warmly. They were led to a spot of high ground dry enough for a camp. Ser Wylis called a halt there, and remained behind with his men to see the fires laid and the horses tended, while his brother Wendel rode on with Catelyn and her uncle to present their father’s respects to their liege lord.
blocks.explanation_zh: out­riders迎接Manderly banner；Wylis扎营，Wendel随Catelyn去见liege lord。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: The ground under their horses’ hooves was soft and wet. It fell away slowly beneath them as they rode past smoky peat fires, lines of horses, and wagons heavy-laden with hardbread and salt beef. On a stony outcrop of land higher than the surrounding country, they passed a lord’s pavilion with walls of heavy sailcloth. Catelyn recognized the banner, the bull moose of the Hornwoods, brown on its dark orange field.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他们穿过湿地camp、peat fires、horses与粮车，经过Hornwood pavilion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Just beyond, through the mists, she glimpsed the walls and towers of Moat Cailin . . . or what remained of them. Immense blocks of black basalt, each as large as a crofter’s cottage, lay scattered and tumbled like a child’s wooden blocks, half-sunk in the soft boggy soil. Nothing else remained of a curtain wall that had once stood as high as Winterfell’s. The wooden keep was gone entirely, rotted away a thousand years past, with not so much as a timber to mark where it had stood. All that was left of the great stronghold of the First Men were three towers . . . three where there had once been twenty, if the taletellers could be believed.
blocks.explanation_zh: fog中Moat Cailin只余散落basalt、腐朽keep遗址与二十塔中的三座。
blocks.literary_note_zh: child’s blocks把巨型basalt ruins缩小为破败玩具，先制造低估。
blocks.background_zh: **Moat Cailin：** Neck北端First Men fortress遗迹，控制唯一可供大军通行的causeway，是North南门。

blocks.original_text: The Gatehouse Tower looked sound enough, and even boasted a few feet of standing wall to either side of it. The Drunkard’s Tower, off in the bog where the south and west walls had once met, leaned like a man about to spew a bellyful of wine into the gutter. And the tall, slender Children’s Tower, where legend said the children of the forest had once called upon their nameless gods to send the hammer of the waters, had lost half its crown. It looked as if some great beast had taken a bite out of the crenellations along the tower top, and spit the rubble across the bog. All three towers were green with moss. A tree was growing out between the stones on the north side of the Gatehouse Tower, its gnarled limbs festooned with ropy white blankets of ghostskin.
blocks.explanation_zh: 三座塔各有残损、倾斜、moss与ghostskin，废墟外观近乎不可守。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Gods have mercy,” Ser Brynden exclaimed when he saw what lay before them. “This is Moat Cailin? It’s no more than a—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Brynden惊讶这就是Moat Cailin，话说一半。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “—death trap,” Catelyn finished. “I know how it looks, Uncle. I thought the same the first time I saw it, but Ned assured me that this ruin is more formidable than it seems. The three surviving towers command the causeway from all sides, and any enemy must pass between them. The bogs here are impenetrable, full of quicksands and suckholes and teeming with snakes. To assault any of the towers, an army would need to wade through waist-deep black muck, cross a moat full of lizard-lions, and scale walls slimy with moss, all the while exposing themselves to fire from archers in the other towers.” She gave her uncle a grim smile. “And when night falls, there are said to be ghosts, cold vengeful spirits of the north who hunger for southron blood.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn接成death trap，解释causeway、bog、quicksand、lizard-lion与交叉arrow fire如何使废墟难攻，并添southron ghosts传说。
blocks.literary_note_zh: death trap反转ruin印象：防御力来自terrain funnel与crossfire而非完整walls。ghosts则是morale folklore。
blocks.background_zh: **terrain defense：** bogs阻止大军展开和重骑迂回，三塔形成互相支援火力，ruins仍可守。

blocks.original_text: Ser Brynden chuckled. “Remind me not to linger here. Last I looked, I was southron myself.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Brynden笑称自己也是southron，不宜久留。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Standards had been raised atop all three towers. The Karstark sunburst hung from the Drunkard’s Tower, beneath the direwolf; on the Children’s Tower it was the Greatjon’s giant in shattered chains. But on the Gatehouse Tower, the Stark banner flew alone. That was where Robb had made his seat. Catelyn made for it, with Ser Brynden and Ser Wendel behind her, their horses stepping slowly down the log-and-plank road that had been laid across the green-and-black fields of mud.
blocks.explanation_zh: 三塔升Karstark、Umber、Stark standards；Robb独占Gatehouse Tower作command seat。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn与Manderly levies抵Moat Cailin；破败三塔因causeway、bog与crossfire仍是North death trap。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She found her son surrounded by his father’s lords bannermen, in a drafty hall with a peat fire smoking in a black hearth. He was seated at a massive stone table, a pile of maps and papers in front of him, talking intently with Roose Bolton and the Greatjon. At first he did not notice her . . . but his wolf did. The great grey beast was lying near the fire, but when Catelyn entered he lifted his head, and his golden eyes met hers. The lords fell silent one by one, and Robb looked up at the sudden quiet and saw her. “Mother?” he said, his voice thick with emotion.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn在smoky hall见Robb与Bolton、Greatjon议map，Grey Wind先发现她，Robb情绪哽咽。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Grey Wind先认出Catelyn，animal perception再次先于human attention。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn wanted to run to him, to kiss his sweet brow, to wrap him in her arms and hold him so tightly that he would never come to harm . . . but here in front of his lords, she dared not. He was playing a man’s part now, and she would not take that away from him. So she held herself at the far end of the basalt slab they were using for a table. The direwolf got to his feet and padded across the room to where she stood. It seemed bigger than a wolf ought to be. “You’ve grown a beard,” she said to Robb, while Grey Wind sniffed her hand.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她想拥抱却为维护Robb在lords前的man role保持距离，只评论他长beard。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn压下embrace是政治表演劳动：母爱若公开过强可能让lords把Robb重新看成child。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: He rubbed his stubbled jaw, suddenly awkward. “Yes.” His chin hairs were redder than the ones on his head.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb摸stubble，忽然拘谨。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I like it.” Catelyn stroked the wolfs head, gently. “It makes you look like my brother Edmure.” Grey Wind nipped at her fingers, playful, and trotted back to his place by the fire.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说beard像Edmure，抚摸Grey Wind，它顽皮轻咬后回火旁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: beard谈话是母子在public gaze下可安全表达亲近的替代语言。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Ser Helman Tallhart was the first to follow the direwolf across the room to pay his respects, kneeling before her and pressing his brow to her hand. “Lady Catelyn,” he said, “you are fair as ever, a welcome sight in troubled times.” The Glovers followed, Galbart and Robett, and Greatjon Umber, and the rest, one by one. Theon Greyjoy was the last. “I had not looked to see you here, my lady,” he said as he knelt.
blocks.explanation_zh: bannermen逐一跪迎，Theon最后且意外她出现。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I had not thought to be here,” Catelyn said, “until I came ashore at White Harbor, and Lord Wyman told me that Robb had called the banners. You know his son, Ser Wendel.” Wendel Manderly stepped forward and bowed as low as his girth would allow. “And my uncle, Ser Brynden Tully, who has left my sister’s service for mine.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说明White Harbor才知Robb召旗，介绍Wendel与离开Lysa投奔自己的Brynden。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “The Blackfish,” Robb said. “Thank you for joining us, ser. We need men of your courage. And you, Ser Wendel, I am glad to have you here. Is Ser Rodrik with you as well, Mother? I’ve missed him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb欢迎Blackfish与Wendel，询问想念的Rodrik是否同行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Ser Rodrik is on his way north from White Harbor. I have named him castellan and commanded him to hold Winterfell till our return. Maester Luwin is a wise counsellor, but unskilled in the arts of war.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说任Rodrik为castellan北返守Winterfell，补Luwin不熟战争。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: **Winterfell command：** Rodrik被任castellan负责军事守城，Luwin继续行政/学识counsel，Bran名义acting lord。

blocks.original_text: “Have no fear on that count, Lady Stark,” the Greatjon told her in his bass rumble. “Winterfell is safe. We’ll shove our swords up Tywin Lannister’s bunghole soon enough, begging your pardons, and then it’s on to the Red Keep to free Ned.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Greatjon粗俗保证Winterfell安全并会击败Tywin、救Ned。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “My lady, a question, as it please you.” Roose Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort, had a small voice, yet when he spoke larger men quieted to listen. His eyes were curiously pale, almost without color, and his look disturbing. “It is said that you hold Lord Tywin’s dwarf son as captive. Have you brought him to us? I vow, we should make good use of such a hostage.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Roose低声问她是否带Tyrion hostage，认为可善加利用。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Roose small voice让larger men quiet，power来自让人主动集中注意而非音量。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I did hold Tyrion Lannister, but no longer,” Catelyn was forced to admit. A chorus of consternation greeted the news. “I was no more pleased than you, my lords. The gods saw fit to free him, with some help from my fool of a sister.” She ought not to be so open in her contempt, she knew, but her parting from the Eyrie had not been pleasant. She had offered to take Lord Robert with her, to foster him at Winterfell for a few years. The company of other boys would do him good, she had dared to suggest. Lysa’s rage had been frightening to behold. “Sister or no,” she had replied, “if you try to steal my son, you will leave by the Moon Door.” After that there was no more to be said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn承认Tyrion获释，引众人不满；她责Lysa愚蠢，并回忆提议foster Robert遭Moon Door威胁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn知道公开contempt Lysa不利却仍说出，显示姐妹决裂的情绪余震。
blocks.background_zh: **Tyrion release：** Lysa允许trial by combat，Bronn获胜后Tyrion依法离开Eyrie，Catelyn失去交换筹码。

blocks.original_text: The lords were anxious to question her further, but Catelyn raised a hand. “No doubt we will have time for all this later, but my journey has fatigued me. I would speak with my son alone. I know you will forgive me, my lords.” She gave them no choice; led by the ever-obliging Lord Hornwood, the bannermen bowed and took their leave. “And you, Theon,” she added when Greyjoy lingered. He smiled and left them.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她以journey疲惫为由强制清场与Robb私谈，也赶走逗留的Theon。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她用fatigue礼貌包装command，gave them no choice显示自己也在行使lordly room control。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: There was ale and cheese on the table. Catelyn tilled a horn, sat, sipped, and studied her son. He seemed taller than when she’d left, and the wisps of beard did make him look older. “Edmure was sixteen when he grew his first whiskers.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 桌上有ale、cheese；她倒酒观察Robb变高、beard使其显老，并提Edmure十六才长whiskers。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 源PDF文本层印作Catelyn tilled a horn，疑为filled但按原样保留。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I will be sixteen soon enough,” Robb said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说自己也快十六。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “And you are fifteen now. Fifteen, and leading a host to battle. Can you understand why I might fear, Robb?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn点明他现十五却领军，问是否理解母亲恐惧。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: His look grew stubborn. “There was no one else.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb固执说没有别人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “No one?” she said. “Pray, who were those men I saw here a moment ago? Roose Bolton, Rickard Karstark, Galbart and Robett Glover, the Greatjon, Helman Tallhart . . . you might have given the command to any of them. Gods be good, you might even have sent Theon, though he would not be my choice.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她列出多名经验lords甚至Theon，指出可委command。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “They are not Starks,” he said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb答他们不是Starks。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “They are men, Robb, seasoned in battle. You were fighting with wooden swords less than a year past.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她说他们至少是battle-seasoned men，而Robb一年前还用wooden swords。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She saw anger in his eyes at that, but it was gone as quick as it came, and suddenly he was a boy again. “I know,” he said, abashed. “Are you . . . are you sending me back to Winterfell?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 怒意掠过后Robb像boy般羞愧，问她是否要送自己回Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 怒意瞬逝、boy again说明Robb lord persona并非虚假，而是可被母亲一句wooden swords刺穿的脆弱层。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn sighed. “I should. You ought never have left. Yet I dare not, not now. You have come too far. Someday these lords will look to you as their liege. If I pack you off now, like a child being sent to bed without his supper, they will remember, and laugh about it in their cups. The day will come when you need them to respect you, even fear you a little. Laughter is poison to fear. I will not do that to you, much as I might wish to keep you safe.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说本该送回却已不能：公开撤换会让未来bannermen嘲笑，laughter会毒害fear与respect。
blocks.literary_note_zh: laughter is poison to fear是feudal authority psychology：撤换本身或许安全，却会长期削弱command legitimacy。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “You have my thanks, Mother,” he said, his relief obvious beneath the formality.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb正式道谢却明显松气。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She reached across his table and touched his hair. “You are my firstborn, Robb. I have only to look at you to remember the day you came into the world, red-faced and squalling.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她摸头说Robb是firstborn，仍记得他出生啼哭。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: He rose, clearly uncomfortable with her touch, and walked to the hearth. Grey Wind rubbed his head against his leg. “You know . . . about Father?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb因母性触碰不适而起身，问她是否知道Eddard消息。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Yes.” The reports of Robert’s sudden death and Ned’s fall had frightened Catelyn more than she could say, but she would not let her son see her fear. “Lord Manderly told me when I landed at White Harbor. Have you had any word of your sisters?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn隐藏恐惧，说Wyman已告知，反问姐妹消息。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她在lords前克制母亲拥抱，介绍援军与Winterfell安排，清场后质问十五岁Robb亲征的authority。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “There was a letter,” Robb said, scratching his direwolf under the jaw. “One for you as well, but it came to Winterfell with mine.” He went to the table, rummaged among some maps and papers, and returned with a crumpled parchment. “This is the one she wrote me, I never thought to bring yours.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb拿出Sansa寄来的皱信，说明另一封给Catelyn但留在Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Something in Robb’s tone troubled her. She smoothed out the paper and read. Concern gave way to disbelief, then to anger, and lastly to fear. “This is Cersei’s letter, not your sister’s,” she said when she was done. “The real message is in what Sansa does not say. All this about how kindly and gently the Lannisters are treating her . . . I know the sound of a threat, even whispered. They have Sansa hostage, and they mean to keep her.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn从措辞读出这是Cersei控制的信；真正消息在Sansa未说之处，过分强调gentle treatment是hostage threat。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn把kind/gentle反向读成威胁，因为hostage不用强调自由；negative space比显性句更可信。
blocks.background_zh: **Sansa letter context：** 信由Cersei council口授、Sansa书写、Varys用Eddard seal盖印；Catelyn凭措辞识别coercion。

blocks.original_text: “There’s no mention of Arya,” Robb pointed out, miserable.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb痛苦指出完全没提Arya。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Arya absence不是死亡证明，却是受控通信中最危险的missing variable。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “No.” Catelyn did not want to think what that might mean, not now, not here.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn不愿此刻推想这意味着什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I had hoped . . . if you still held the Imp, a trade of hostages . . . ” He took Sansa’s letter and crumpled it in his fist, and she could tell from the way he did it that it was not the first time. “Is there word from the Eyrie? I wrote to Aunt Lysa, asking help. Has she called Lord Arryn’s banners, do you know? Will the knights of the Vale come join us?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb原盼用Tyrion换hostages，又问Lysa是否召Vale banners相助。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Only one,” she said, “the best of them, my uncle . . . but Brynden Blackfish was a Tully first. My sister is not about to stir beyond her Bloody Gate.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说只得到Brynden一人，Lysa不会越Bloody Gate。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: **Vale neutrality：** Lysa掌Bloody Gate与Vale knights，选择不出兵；Brynden以个人/Tully身份加入。

blocks.original_text: Robb took it hard. “Mother, what are we going to do? I brought this whole army together, eighteen thousand men, but I don’t . . . I’m not certain . . . ” He looked to her, his eyes shining, the proud young lord melted away in an instant, and quick as that he was a child again, a fifteen-year-old boy looking to his mother for answers.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb受打击，承认虽集一万八千men却不确定怎么办，young lord表象融化成求母亲答案的boy。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 一万八千与上一章一万二千差异来自后续沿路joiners/Manderly等集结，文本未在此逐项重算。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: It would not do.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn判断不能让他继续依赖。
blocks.literary_note_zh: It would not do短句是Catelyn自我切换：母亲想安慰，advisor必须逼commander独立。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “What are you so afraid of, Robb?” she asked gently.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她温和问Robb最怕什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I . . . ” He turned his head away, to hide the first tear. “If we march . . . even if we win . . . the Lannisters hold Sansa, and Father. They’ll kill them, won’t they?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb藏泪说即使出兵取胜，Lannisters也可能杀Sansa与Eddard。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “They want us to think so.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说敌人正想让他们如此认为。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “You mean they’re lying?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb问是否是lying。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I do not know, Robb. What I do know is that you have no choice. If you go to King’s Landing and swear fealty, you will never be allowed to leave. If you turn your tail and retreat to Winterfell, your lords will lose all respect for you. Some may even go over to the Lannisters. Then the queen, with that much less to fear, can do as she likes with her prisoners. Our best hope, our only true hope, is that you can defeat the foe in the field. If you should chance to take Lord Tywin or the Kingslayer captive, why then a trade might very well be possible, but that is not the heart of it. So long as you have power enough that they must fear you, Ned and your sister should be safe. Cersei is wise enough to know that she may need them to make her peace, should the fighting go against her.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn坦言不知道，但投降会被扣、撤退会失尊重甚至失封臣；只有field victory与保持power才能让hostages有谈判价值。
blocks.literary_note_zh: power enough that they must fear you把hostage safety建立在deterrence而非敌人仁慈；trade只是可能工具。
blocks.background_zh: **hostage deterrence：** prisoners活着可交换、迫降或谈peace；若对方失去军事威胁，hostage价值可能下降。

blocks.original_text: “What if the fighting doesn’t go against her?” Robb asked. “What if it goes against us?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb问若战争对己方不利。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn读出Sansa hostage letter，确认Arya失联、Vale不援；她以deterrence逻辑说明Robb只能field victory。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn took his hand. “Robb, I will not soften the truth for you. If you lose, there is no hope for any of us. They say there is naught but stone at the heart of Casterly Rock. Remember the fate of Rhaegar’s children.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn不粉饰：若败，全家无希望，并以Rhaegar children命运警告。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Rhaegar children指capital被攻破后被杀，Catelyn用历史最坏结果拒绝虚假安慰。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She saw the fear in his young eyes then, but there was a strength as well. “Then I will not lose,” he vowed.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb眼中有fear也有strength，誓言不会输。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Then I will not lose是决心，不是strategy；Catelyn立刻转入考问避免口号替代能力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Tell me what you know of the fighting in the riverlands,” she said. She had to learn if he was truly ready.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn转考问riverlands战况，判断他是否真准备好。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Less than a fortnight past, they fought a battle in the hills below the Golden Tooth,” Robb said. “Uncle Edmure had sent Lord Vance and Lord Piper to hold the pass, but the Kingslayer descended on them and put them to flight. Lord Vance was slain. The last word we had was that Lord Piper was falling back to join your brother and his other bannermen at Riverrun, with Jaime Lannister on his heels. That’s not the worst of it, though. All the time they were battling in the pass, Lord Tywin was bringing a second Lannister army around from the south. It’s said to be even larger than Jaime’s host.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说Jaime在Golden Tooth击败Vance、Piper，Vance死、Piper退向Riverrun；同时Tywin另率更大army北上。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb信息按两支Lannister armies组织，显示已掌握operational picture而非只知family危机。
blocks.background_zh: **two Lannister hosts：** Jaime压Riverrun，Tywin从south北进控制kingsroad，形成潜在夹击。

blocks.original_text: “Father must have known that, because he sent out some men to oppose them, under the king’s own banner. He gave the command to some southron lordling, Lord Erik or Derik or something like that, but Ser Raymun Darry rode with him, and the letter said there were other knights as well, and a force of Father’s own guardsmen. Only it was a trap. Lord Derik had no sooner crossed the Red Fork than the Lannisters fell upon him, the king’s banner be damned, and Gregor Clegane took them in the rear as they tried to pull back across the Mummer’s Ford. This Lord Derik and a few others may have escaped, no one is certain, but Ser Raymun was killed, and most of our men from Winterfell. Lord Tywin has closed off the kingsroad, it’s said, and now he’s marching north toward Harrenhal, burning as he goes.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard派king’s banner force被伏击，Gregor从rear袭Mummer’s Ford，Raymun与多数Winterfell men死，Tywin封kingsroad焚掠北进。
blocks.literary_note_zh: king’s banner未阻止伏击，说明legal authority已失去跨阵营约束力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Grim and grimmer, thought Catelyn. It was worse than she’d imagined. “You mean to meet him here?” she asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn觉得比预想更糟，问是否在Moat Cailin迎战。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “If he comes so far, but no one thinks he will,” Robb said. “I’ve sent word to Howland Reed, Father’s old friend at Greywater Watch. If the Lannisters come up the Neck, the crannogmen will bleed them every step of the way, but Galbart Glover says Lord Tywin is too smart for that, and Roose Bolton agrees. He’ll stay close to the Trident, they believe, taking the castles of the river lords one by one, until Riverrun stands alone. We need to march south to meet him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说若Tywin来便守，但已通知Howland。 他续说crannogmen可在Neck消耗Lannisters，但Glover、Bolton认为Tywin会逐城孤立Riverrun，因此必须南march。
blocks.literary_note_zh: p.549–550为同一Robb话段；Howland Reed与crannogmen是Neck防御网络。
blocks.background_zh: **Howland Reed：** Greywater Watch lord、Eddard old friend，crannogmen熟悉Neck swamp guerrilla。

blocks.original_text: The very idea of it chilled Catelyn to the bone. What chance would a fifteen-year-old boy have against seasoned battle commanders like Jaime and Tywin Lannister? “Is that wise? You are strongly placed here. It’s said that the old Kings in the North could stand at Moat Cailin and throw back hosts ten times the size of their own.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn想到十五岁Robb对Tywin、Jaime而胆寒，提出Moat可挡十倍军力。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Yes, but our food and supplies are running low, and this is not land we can live off easily. We’ve been waiting for Lord Manderly, but now that his sons have joined us, we need to march.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说食物不足、沼地不能养军，Manderlys已到所以必须走。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She was hearing the lords bannermen speaking with her son’s voice, she realized. Over the years, she had hosted many of them at Winterfell, and been welcomed with Ned to their own hearths and tables. She knew what sorts of men they were, each one. She wondered if Robb did.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn听出bannermen意见借Robb voice说出，怀疑他是否真正了解这些men。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn识别borrowed voices，提醒young leader吸收advice时可能把consensus误当自身judgment。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: And yet there was sense in what they said. This host her son had assembled was not a standing army such as the Free Cities were accustomed to maintain, nor a force of guardsmen paid in coin. Most of them were smallfolk: crofters, fieldhands, fishermen, sheepherders, the sons of innkeeps and traders and tanners, leavened with a smattering of sellswords and freeriders hungry for plunder. When their lords called, they came . . . but not forever. “Marching is all very well,” she said to her son, “but where, and to what purpose? What do you mean to do?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她仍承认逻辑：army由暂时应召smallfolk组成，不能无限驻扎；追问march目的与行动方案。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 非standing army说明时间成本：smallfolk离田、粮食消耗、忠诚期限都会迫使行动。
blocks.background_zh: **feudal levy：** 多为temporarily called smallfolk而非职业军，粮食、农时与lord loyalty限制集结时间。

blocks.original_text: Robb hesitated. “The Greatjon thinks we should take the battle to Lord Tywin and surprise him,” he said, “but the Glovers and the Karstarks feel we’d be wiser to go around his army and join up with Uncle Ser Edmure against the Kingslayer.” He ran his fingers through his shaggy mane of auburn hair, looking unhappy. “Though by the time we reach Riverrun . . . I’m not certain . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说Greatjon主张突袭Tywin，Glovers/Karstarks主张绕行会合Edmure对Jaime，自己尚未确定。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Be certain,” Catelyn told her son, “or go home and take up that wooden sword again. You cannot afford to seem indecisive in front of men like Roose Bolton and Rickard Karstark. Make no mistake, Robb—these are your bannermen, not your friends. You named yourself battle commander. Command.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn严厉要求确定，否则回家练木剑；bannermen非friends，既自任commander就必须command。
blocks.literary_note_zh: bannermen not friends不是否定私人关系，而是强调他们有House interests且会评估弱点。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Her son looked at her, startled, as if he could not credit what he was hearing. “As you say, Mother.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb惊讶后接受。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb准确汇报riverlands两支Lannister armies；粮食与levy期限迫使南march，Catelyn要求他真正command。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I’ll ask you again. What do you mean to do?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她再次问具体计划。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Robb drew a map across the table, a ragged piece of old leather covered with lines of faded paint. One end curled up from being rolled; he weighed it down with his dagger. “Both plans have virtues, but . . . look, if we try to swing around Lord Tywin’s host, we take the risk of being caught between him and the Kingslayer, and if we attack him . . . by all reports, he has more men than I do, and a lot more armored horse. The Greatjon says that won’t matter if we catch him with his breeches down, but it seems to me that a man who has fought as many battles as Tywin Lannister won’t be so easily surprised.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb铺map分析两方案风险：绕Tywin可能夹击，正攻则兵少骑弱且Tywin难被surprise。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb拒绝Greatjon‘catch breeches down’乐观，显示能以enemy competence校正己方confidence。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Good,” she said. She could hear echoes of Ned in his voice, as he sat there, puzzling over the map. “Tell me more.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn听见Eddard echoes，称赞并让他继续。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I’d leave a small force here to hold Moat Cailin, archers mostly, and march the rest down the causeway,” he said, “but once we’re below the Neck, I’d split our host in two. The foot can continue down the kingsroad, while our horsemen cross the Green Fork at the Twins.” He pointed. “When Lord Tywin gets word that we’ve come south, he’ll march north to engage our main host, leaving our riders free to hurry down the west bank to Riverrun.” Robb sat back, not quite daring to smile, but pleased with himself and hungry for her praise.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb计划留archers守Moat，南出Neck后分军：foot走kingsroad吸引Tywin，horse过Twins沿west bank救Riverrun。
blocks.literary_note_zh: split plan让foot成为visible main host吸引Tywin，horse以bridge access完成operational maneuver。
blocks.background_zh: **split plan：** infantry沿kingsroad吸引Tywin；cavalry需借Twins过Green Fork，从west bank赶赴Riverrun。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn frowned down at the map. “You’d put a river between the two parts of your army.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn指出这样会以river隔开己方两军。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn首先指出river隔己方，履行red-team role而非立即赞美。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “And between Jaime and Lord Tywin,” he said eagerly. The smile came at last. “There’s no crossing on the Green Fork above the ruby ford, where Robert won his crown. Not until the Twins, all the way up here, and Lord Frey controls that bridge. He’s your father’s bannerman, isn’t that so?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb说river也隔开Jaime与Tywin；Green Fork上游只有Frey控制的Twins bridge，并问Frey是否Tully bannerman。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb的反转是river也隔enemy，关键条件变为Frey bridge cooperation。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: The Late Lord Frey, Catelyn thought. “He is,” she admitted, “but my father has never trusted him. Nor should you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn想到Late Lord Frey，承认其臣属却警告父亲从不信他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Late Lord Frey是其迟到战场的旧绰号；formal bannerman status不等于可靠obedience。
blocks.background_zh: **Walder Frey：** Hoster bannerman且掌唯一近便bridge，但长期以迟到、算计闻名，cooperation不可假定。

blocks.original_text: “I won’t,” Robb promised. “What do you think?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb保证谨慎并问评价。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: She was impressed despite herself. He looks like a Tully, she thought, yet he’s still his father’s son, and Ned taught him well. “Which force would you command?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn虽 impressed，追问他亲领哪支。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “The horse,” he answered at once. Again like his father; Ned would always take the more dangerous task himself.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb立即选更危险机动的horse。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb选horse重复Eddard把dangerous task留给自己，既建立leadership也增加personal risk。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “And the other?”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她问另一支谁领。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “The Greatjon is always saying that we should smash Lord Tywin. I thought I’d give him the honor.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb想把对Tywin的honor给一直主张正攻的Greatjon。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: It was his first misstep, but how to make him see it without wounding his fledgling confidence? “Your father once told me that the Greatjon was as fearless as any man he had ever known.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn认为这是首个失误，需不伤其confidence地纠正，以Eddard称Greatjon fearless开场。
blocks.literary_note_zh: first misstep不是选择分军，而是把需要cunning的eastern command给fearless Greatjon。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Robb grinned. “Grey Wind ate two of his fingers, and he laughed about it. So you agree, then?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb以Grey Wind断指事件说明Greatjon fearless，以为母亲同意。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Your father is not fearless,” Catelyn pointed out. “He is brave, but that is very different.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn指出Eddard并非fearless而是brave，两者不同。
blocks.literary_note_zh: fearless缺少风险感知，brave则看见fear仍行动；Catelyn用Eddard作为对照定义。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Her son considered that for a moment. “The eastern host will be all that stands between Lord Tywin and Winterfell,” he said thoughtfully. “Well, them and whatever few bowmen I leave here at the Moat. So I don’t want someone fearless, do I?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb思考后明白eastern host护North，不该由只会无惧进攻者指挥。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “No. You want cold cunning, I should think, not courage.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn说需要cold cunning而非courage。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “Roose Bolton,” Robb said at once. “That man scares me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb立即选择令自己害怕的Roose Bolton。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robb从cold cunning立即想到令自己害怕的Roose，把personal affect转成enemy-impact criterion。
blocks.background_zh: **Roose choice：** pale、quiet Dreadfort lord令Robb不安；Catelyn认为这种coldness更适合牵制Tywin。

blocks.original_text: “Then let us pray he will scare Tywin Lannister as well.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn希望Roose也能吓到Tywin。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Robb nodded and rolled up the map. “I’ll give the commands, and assemble an escort to take you home to Winterfell.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robb收map准备下令，并安排escort送Catelyn回Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 母子推演split-army plan、Twins bridge与两路commander；确定Robb领horse、Roose领foot后，Catelyn决定随军赴Riverrun。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn had fought to keep herself strong, for Ned’s sake and for this stubborn brave son of theirs. She had put despair and fear aside, as if they were garments she did not choose to wear . . . but now she saw that she had donned them after all.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn一直把despair、fear像不穿的衣物般搁置，此刻发现其实已穿上。
blocks.literary_note_zh: garments metaphor显示情绪并非真能脱下；她的control一直建立在压住而非消除despair。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。

blocks.original_text: “I am not going to Winterfell,” she heard herself say, surprised at the sudden rush of tears that blurred her vision. “My father may be dying behind the walls of Riverrun. My brother is surrounded by foes. I must go to them.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 她落泪说不回Winterfell：Hoster或将死、Edmure被围，她必须去Riverrun。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 突然泪水来自family roles同时压回：Ned、Robb、Hoster、Edmure使她无法退到safe rear。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Moat Cailin地理、feudal army约束、hostage deterrence与Robb command development。
